commands.description:

clear

options.name: --json

commands.description:
Output current project/environment (for use in shell

Create a .bella file in the current directory (like 
git init).

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show active context with source information.

commands.name: use

options.name: --shell
options.description:
Shell syntax for output: bash (default), fish, 
powershell

arguments.name: context
arguments.description:
Context in the form 
<project>/<env>, or empty to clear. Example: camino/dev

commands.description:
Set an ephemeral session context (no file written). 
Requires shell function from 'bella shell init'.

Directory-aware Bella project/environment context (for 
shell prompts).

commands.name: env

options.name: --json
options.description: Output as JSON (key-value pairs, for scripting).

options.name: --shell
options.description: Shell syntax: bash (default), fish, powershell

options.name: --unset
options.description:
Output unset/Remove-Item statements to clear all Bella
vars.

arguments.name: context
arguments.description:
Optional context override: 
<project>/<env>. Falls back to .bella file / global 
default.

commands.description:
Output eval-able export statements for 
BELLA_BAXTER_PROJECT, BELLA_BAXTER_ENV (and legacy BELLA_PROJECT/BELLA_ENV 
aliases). Usage: eval $(bella env)

commands.name: exec

options.name: --app
options.description:
Application name injected as BELLA_BAXTER_APP_CLIENT 
(useful for audit logs)

options.name: --environment
options.description:
Environment slug — used for workload identity 
(GitHub Actions / Kubernetes)

options.name: --project
options.description:
Project slug — used for workload identity (GitHub
Actions / Kubernetes)

arguments.name: cmd...
arguments.description: Command and arguments to run (prefix with --)

commands.description:
Inject Bella connection credentials (API key + URL) 
and run a command. The subprocess uses the Bella SDK to discover its 
project/environment context from the API key.

commands.name: generate

options.name: --exclude-ambiguous
options.description: Exclude ambiguous characters (0, O, 1, l, I)

options.name: --length
options.description: Password length (default: 20, range: 8-128)

options.name: --memorable
options.description:
Generate a memorable passphrase instead of a random 
password

options.name: --no-numbers
options.description: Exclude numbers

options.name: --no-symbols
options.description: Exclude symbols

options.name: --no-uppercase
options.description: Exclude uppercase letters

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
Output only the value (no labels, for piping to 
clipboard etc.)

options.name: --separator
options.description:
Passphrase separator: 
hyphens|spaces|periods|commas|underscores (default: hyphens)

options.name: --words
options.description:
Number of words for passphrase (default: 5, range: 
3-8)

commands.description:
Generate a cryptographically secure random password or 
passphrase.

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create a .bella context file in the current directory 
(shortcut for 'bella context init').

commands.name: issue

options.name: --environment
options.description:
Environment slug. Falls back to context (.bella file /
BELLA_BAXTER_ENV env var)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Output format: token (default, just the token string) 
or json (full response)

options.name: --project
options.description:
Project slug. Falls back to context (.bella file / 
BELLA_BAXTER_PROJECT env var)

options.name: --reason
options.description:
Human-readable reason / client name for audit 
purposes

options.name: --scope
options.description:
Required. Comma-separated list of scope names (e.g. 
stripe,payment)

options.name: --ttl
options.description: Token lifetime in minutes (default: 15, max: 480)

commands.description:
Issue a short-lived scoped API token for a specific 
environment.

commands.name: login
commands.description: Log in to Bella Baxter (browser OAuth2 or API key).

commands.name: logout
commands.description: Log out and clear stored credentials.

commands.name: mcp

options.name: --api-url
options.description: Override Bella API base URL.

options.name: --print-config
options.description:
Print Claude Desktop and VS Code MCP config snippets 
then exit.

commands.description:
Start the Bella Baxter MCP server (stdio) for Claude 
Desktop / VS Code Copilot.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all orgs you belong to.

arguments.name: slug-or-id
arguments.description: The org slug or ID to switch to

options.name: --tag
options.description: Add a tag (can be specified multiple times)

options.name: --tag
options.description: Filter projects by tag

options.name: --add-tag
options.description: Add a tag (can be specified multiple times)

options.name: --remove-tag
options.description: Remove a tag (can be specified multiple times)

options.name: --set-tags
options.description:
Replace all tags with a comma-separated list (use 
empty string to clear all)

commands.name: pull
commands.description:
Download all secrets and write to a file (default: .env). 
Shorthand for 'bella secrets get -o .env'.

commands.name: run

options.name: --app
options.description:
Application name injected as BELLA_BAXTER_APP_CLIENT 
(useful for audit logs)

options.name: --environment
options.description: Environment slug or ID

options.name: --poll-interval
options.description:
Polling interval in seconds for --watch (default: 
30)

options.name: --project
options.description: Project slug or ID

options.name: --provider
options.description: Provider name to use (if environment has multiple)

options.name: --signal
options.description: How to reload on change: restart (default) or sighup

options.name: --watch
options.description: Watch for secret changes and reload the process

arguments.name: cmd...
arguments.description: Command and arguments to run (prefix with --)

commands.description: Inject Bella secrets as env vars and run a command.

commands.name: generate

options.name: --types
options.description:
Generate a bella-secrets.ts TypeScript module 
augmentation file for @bella-baxter/config SDK (enables app.bella.DATABASE_URL 
typed property access). Exports BELLA_COERCIONS for runtime type coercion.

commands.description:
Generate typed secrets code for dotnet, python, go, 
typescript, dart, php, ruby, or swift.

arguments.description:

powerlevel10k, oh-my-posh, starship, fish, powershell

commands.description:
Output shell integration snippet for your prompt 
framework.

commands.name: open

options.name: --shell
options.description:
Shell to spawn (default: $SHELL on Unix, cmd.exe 
on Windows). Example: /bin/bash, zsh, powershell

arguments.name: context
arguments.description:
Optional context override: 
<project>/<env>. Falls back to .bella file / global 
default.

commands.description:
Spawn an interactive subshell with BELLA_API_KEY, 
BELLA_PROJECT, BELLA_ENV pre-set.

commands.name: ssh
ca-key

commands.name: configure
commands.description: Configure SSH CA for an environment (admin only).

commands.name: connect

options.name: --key
options.description:
Path to SSH private key (without .pub). Default: 
~/.ssh/id_ed25519 or id_rsa

commands.description: Sign key (if needed) and open an SSH connection.

options.name: --allowed-users
options.description:
Comma-separated allowed users (e.g. 
ec2-user,ubuntu)

options.name: --default-ttl
options.description: Default certificate TTL (e.g. 8h)

options.name: --max-ttl
options.description: Max certificate TTL (e.g. 24h)

commands.name: list
commands.description: List SSH roles for an environment.

commands.name: sign

options.name: --key
options.description:
Path to SSH public key file (default: 
~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub or id_rsa.pub)

options.name: --principals
options.description: Comma-separated valid principals

options.name: --ttl
options.description: Certificate TTL (e.g. 1h)

commands.description: Sign your SSH public key and write the certificate.
Manage SSH certificates via Vault SSH CA.

commands.name: totp
code
commands.description: Get the current TOTP code for a key.

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import a TOTP key from an otpauth:// URL.

commands.name: list

Manage TOTP/2FA keys stored in environments.
